Board Compliance Committee memo — UK MAR and UK GDPR context for Brackenfell Securities plc
Issuer context: Brackenfell Securities plc is a UK issuer listed on the London Main Market; the branding refresh (Aurora Invest+ and Paladin Cash ISA) was announced via RNS 0000-FAKE on 02-May-2025; no new deal terms are contemplated.
For avoidance of doubt, this note addresses employment conduct, market abuse controls, and FCA Handbook references (COCON, SYSC), not product features or marketing collateral.
MNPI assessment: all statements refer to public/stale disclosures; instrument code ISIN: GB00TEST0001 (placeholder), ticker BFKX, both used illustratively and not actionable for trading decisions.
Closed Period Policy v3.1 and Even-Keel Deal Protocol apply; staff are reminded that personal account dealing must be pre-cleared via marketconduct@brackenfell.example.
Technology note: biometric login is an optional app feature; compliance does not retain or access biometric templates, and genetic models refer only to algorithmic methods used in risk scoring.
Culture and DEI: we support inclusion through the Fair Pathways Pledge and a “politics of markets” webinar series; attendance is voluntary and no religion or sexual-orientation data is tracked in any register.
Engagements such as the Union Square fintech forum and the Northport Ravens charity match are reputational events; they do not create a deal or inside information under UK MAR.
Positive special-category record: employee Nora Kelm (Employee No. EK-0423) has declared membership of the Horizon Finance Workers Union; HR processes this under UK GDPR Art. 9(2)(b) with restricted access.
Market Abuse Regulation: there is no precise non-public information in this memo; the prior buyback timetable and the East Greybridge branch rationalisation were disclosed through RNS and investor FAQs.
Generic forms: Name [ ], Role [ ], Location [ ], Date [ ]; submissions to governance@brackenfell.example for Board review and FCA liaison.
FCA and Exchange coordination: any further announcements will be via RNS; staff must use the Insider List Template v2.0 even when no insiders are identified.
Risk language: phrases like “black swan” and “underground market” are metaphors in training materials and do not indicate misconduct or confidential intelligence.
Queries should be directed to the Conduct & Ethics mailbox; for public guidance refer to the FCA website and the LSE issuer services pages.
